We apply scaling augmentation to the student’s inputs.
As training proceeds, we follow a schedule to increase the scale of the student augmentation.
The scale of student augmentation is increased until it equals that of the original image, allowing the student to focus on smaller features earlier in the training process.
The scale is iteratively increased to encourage the teacher to make more accurate predictions on smaller scale objects in the later stages of training.
Increasing the scale of the student's inputs with minimal errors due to scale is facilitated by the accurate small scale pseudo-labels.
A gaussian function for the scaling factor is applied based on the schedule values to ensure the knowledge of the previous scales is retained.

The proposed framework, 2PCNet, presents a novel solution to the challenges of day-to-night domain adaptive object detection.
With the Two-Phase Consistency approach of 2PCNet, high and low confidence knowledge can be effectively leveraged for the student.
The Two-Phase Consistency approach of 2PCNet effectively leverages high and low confidence knowledge for the student, mitigating error propagation.
2PCNet addresses issues arising from small scale objects through the use of student-scaling.
2PCNet addresses issues arising from dark objects through the use of NightAug.
Experimental results on the e BDD100K and SHIFT datasets demonstrate that 2PCNet outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods.
The proposed framework provides an effective and efficient solution for day-to-night domain adaptive object detection.
